title: Exponential expressivity in deep neural networks through transient chaos
authors: Poole, B.; Lahiri, S.; Raghu, M.; Sohl-Dickstein, J.; Ganguli, S.
key_finding: Random deep networks undergo an order-to-chaos phase transition as weight variance crosses a critical value; near the transition, expressivity grows exponentially with depth while correlations decay slowly
hypothesis_generated: Edge-of-chaos initialisation maximises expressive capacity; check whether trained nets stay near this boundary or drift

title: Deep information propagation
authors: Schoenholz, S.S.; Gilmer, J.; Ganguli, S.; Sohl-Dickstein, J.
key_finding: Initialising at the edge-of-chaos boundary (q* fixed point with chi=1) yields vanishing gradient-correlation decay and enables training of very deep networks
hypothesis_generated: Depth of trainable network diverges at criticality; trained nets may maintain residual edge-of-chaos structure

title: Dynamical isometry and a mean field theory of CNNs
authors: Xiao, L.; Bahri, Y.; Sohl-Dickstein, J.; Schoenholz, S.S.; Pennington, J.
key_finding: Delta-orthogonal initialisation for CNNs achieves dynamical isometry allowing trainability of 10000-layer vanilla CNNs without batchnorm or residuals
hypothesis_generated: CNNs can be placed at edge of chaos without topology tricks; contrast with skip-connection regimes

title: Tensor Programs I-IV: architecture-agnostic infinite-width limits
authors: Yang, G.
key_finding: Unifies infinite-width limits (GP, NTK, feature-learning) for arbitrary architectures via tensor-program formalism
hypothesis_generated: Provides mean-field predictions against which to compare finite-width criticality measurements

title: Resurrecting the sigmoid in deep learning through dynamical isometry
authors: Pennington, J.; Schoenholz, S.S.; Ganguli, S.
key_finding: Free-probability analysis of Jacobian spectrum shows orthogonal init + sigmoid can train very deep nets; controls entire singular-value distribution
hypothesis_generated: Jacobian spectrum is a direct criticality observable; can compute on trained transformers

title: Deep neural networks as Gaussian processes
authors: Lee, J.; Bahri, Y.; Novak, R.; Schoenholz, S.S.; Pennington, J.; Sohl-Dickstein, J.
key_finding: Infinite-width Bayesian neural networks are equivalent to GPs with a specific recursive kernel
hypothesis_generated: Baseline for what 'untrained' criticality looks like in the infinite-width limit

title: Neural tangent kernel: convergence and generalization in neural networks
authors: Jacot, A.; Gabriel, F.; Hongler, C.
key_finding: In the infinite-width limit, GD training evolves via a fixed kernel (NTK); dynamics become linear
hypothesis_generated: Lazy regime is incompatible with non-trivial feature learning; finite-width departures relate to criticality

title: On exact computation with an infinitely wide neural net
authors: Arora, S.; Du, S.S.; Hu, W.; Li, Z.; Salakhutdinov, R.; Wang, R.
key_finding: Closed-form NTK computation for CNNs; exact kernel enables comparison with finite-width trained nets
hypothesis_generated: Use NTK regression as null model for what a non-critical network would predict

title: Wide neural networks of any depth evolve as linear models under gradient descent
authors: Lee, J.; Xiao, L.; Schoenholz, S.S.; Bahri, Y.; Novak, R.; Sohl-Dickstein, J.; Pennington, J.
key_finding: Wide finite nets are well-approximated by their linearisation around init throughout training
hypothesis_generated: Edge of chaos at init determines linearised dynamics throughout training; test whether trained nonlinear features cluster near chi=1

title: Feature learning in infinite-width neural networks (muP)
authors: Yang, G.; Hu, E.J.
key_finding: Maximal Update Parametrisation gives a distinct infinite-width limit (feature-learning) that enables hyperparameter transfer across scales
hypothesis_generated: muP networks deliberately leave the NTK/lazy regime — are they closer to or further from edge of chaos?

title: Grokking: generalization beyond overfitting on small algorithmic datasets
authors: Power, A.; Burda, Y.; Edwards, H.; Babuschkin, I.; Misra, V.
key_finding: Networks can transition abruptly from memorisation to generalisation long after training loss converges
hypothesis_generated: Phase transition in weights; is there a concomitant signature in activation criticality / avalanche statistics?

title: Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
authors: Nanda, N.; Chan, L.; Liberum, T.; Smith, J.; Steinhardt, J.
key_finding: Grokking in modular addition is explained by a discrete Fourier-basis circuit that emerges during training; specific progress measures predict the transition
hypothesis_generated: Phase transitions in interpretability metrics align with weight-norm transitions; look for analogous activation-criticality transitions

title: Towards understanding grokking: an effective theory of representation learning
authors: Liu, Z.; Kitouni, O.; Nolte, N.; Michaud, E.J.; Tegmark, M.; Williams, M.
key_finding: Phase diagram of grokking in four regimes; weight decay and data fraction control the transition
hypothesis_generated: Provides phase-diagram template for mapping criticality vs topology knobs

title: Gradient descent on neural networks typically occurs at the edge of stability
authors: Cohen, J.M.; Kaur, S.; Li, Y.; Kolter, J.Z.; Talwalkar, A.
key_finding: Hessian top eigenvalue hovers near 2/lr during typical GD training; loss is non-monotone yet training progresses
hypothesis_generated: Edge-of-stability is an optimisation-dynamics criticality distinct from signal-propagation criticality; both can coexist

title: Self-stabilization: the implicit bias of gradient descent at the edge of stability
authors: Damian, A.; Nichani, E.; Lee, J.D.
key_finding: Analytic mechanism by which GD self-regulates the sharpness to hover near 2/lr
hypothesis_generated: Self-organising behaviour in optimiser is a criticality analogue; do activations also self-organise?

title: Adaptive gradient methods at the edge of stability
authors: Cohen, J.M.; Ghorbani, B.; Krishnan, S.; Agarwal, N.; Medapati, S.; Badura, M.; Suo, D.; Cardoze, D.; Nado, Z.; Dahl, G.E.; Gilmer, J.
key_finding: Adam and related adaptive optimisers exhibit an analogous 'edge of stability' via the preconditioned Hessian
hypothesis_generated: Edge-of-stability is robust across optimisers used in LLM training; relevant for Adam-trained nanoGPT

title: The lottery ticket hypothesis: finding sparse trainable neural networks
authors: Frankle, J.; Carbin, M.
key_finding: Dense randomly-initialised nets contain sparse subnetworks (winning tickets) that, trained in isolation with original init, match full-net performance
hypothesis_generated: Topology and init jointly determine trainability; winning-ticket subnet could be examined for criticality signatures

title: Scaling laws for neural language models
authors: Kaplan, J.; McCandlish, S.; Henighan, T.; Brown, T.B.; Chess, B.; Child, R.; Gray, S.; Radford, A.; Wu, J.; Amodei, D.
key_finding: Test loss scales as a power law in model size, dataset size, and compute with specific exponents
hypothesis_generated: Power-law scaling in macroscopic quantities; is this an echo of underlying critical organisation?

title: Training compute-optimal large language models (Chinchilla)
authors: Hoffmann, J.; Borgeaud, S.; Mensch, A.; Buchatskaya, E.; Cai, T.; Rutherford, E.; de las Casas, D.; Hendricks, L.A.; Welbl, J.; Clark, A.; Hennigan, T.; Noland, E.; Millican, K.; van den Driessche, G.; Damoc, B.; Guy, A.; Osindero, S.; Simonyan, K.; Elsen, E.; Rae, J.W.; Vinyals, O.; Sifre, L.
key_finding: Compute-optimal training requires model and data to scale ~equally; Kaplan exponents revised
hypothesis_generated: Use Chinchilla-optimal loss as reference for 'healthy' scaling; deviation may flag off-critical training

title: Explaining neural scaling laws
authors: Bahri, Y.; Dyer, E.; Kaplan, J.; Lee, J.; Sharma, U.
key_finding: Four scaling regimes explained: variance-limited, resolution-limited, with data and parameter exponents tied to data manifold dimension and kernel spectrum
hypothesis_generated: Connects scaling exponents to spectral properties of NTK — criticality-like universality

title: Emergent abilities of large language models
authors: Wei, J.; Tay, Y.; Bommasani, R.; Raffel, C.; Barret, Z.; Metzler, D.; Chi, E.H.; Hashimoto, T.; Vinyals, O.; Liang, P.; Dean, J.; Fedus, W.
key_finding: Specific capabilities (arithmetic, instruction-following) appear abruptly above scale thresholds
hypothesis_generated: Emergent-capability boundaries could correspond to activation-criticality phase transitions; test on nanoGPT size sweep

title: Are emergent abilities of large language models a mirage?
authors: Schaeffer, R.; Miranda, B.; Koyejo, S.
key_finding: Most emergent abilities vanish under smooth metrics; argue emergence is an artefact of discontinuous metrics
hypothesis_generated: Methodological warning: critical-looking transitions may be metric artefacts; use smooth order parameters

title: A mathematical framework for transformer circuits
authors: Elhage, N.; Nanda, N.; Olsson, C.; Henighan, T.; Joseph, N.; Mann, B.; Askell, A.; Bai, Y.; Chen, A.; Conerly, T.; DasSarma, N.; Drain, D.; Ganguli, D.; Hatfield-Dodds, Z.; Hernandez, D.; Jones, A.; Kernion, J.; Lovitt, L.; Ndousse, K.; Amodei, D.; Brown, T.; Clark, J.; Kaplan, J.; McCandlish, S.; Olah, C.
key_finding: Decomposes attention-only transformers into QK/OV circuits and virtual-head pathways; residual stream as communication channel
hypothesis_generated: Residual stream is the natural substrate on which to compute avalanche/correlation statistics

title: In-context learning and induction heads
authors: Olsson, C.; Elhage, N.; Nanda, N.; Joseph, N.; DasSarma, N.; Henighan, T.; Mann, B.; Askell, A.; Bai, Y.; Chen, A.; Conerly, T.; Drain, D.; Ganguli, D.; Hatfield-Dodds, Z.; Hernandez, D.; Johnston, S.; Jones, A.; Kernion, J.; Lovitt, L.; Ndousse, K.; Amodei, D.; Brown, T.; Clark, J.; Kaplan, J.; McCandlish, S.; Olah, C.
key_finding: Induction heads emerge via a phase transition during training; responsible for much in-context learning
hypothesis_generated: Mid-training circuit emergence is a concrete phase transition — align with criticality measurements on nanoGPT

title: Interpretability in the wild: a circuit for indirect object identification
authors: Wang, K.; Variengien, A.; Conmy, A.; Shlegeris, B.; Steinhardt, J.
key_finding: Reverse-engineers a specific 26-head circuit in GPT-2 small implementing the IOI task
hypothesis_generated: Demonstrates sparse functional circuits in pretrained GPT-2 — evidence of highly organised (possibly scale-free) usage

title: Towards monosemanticity: decomposing language models with dictionary learning
authors: Bricken, T.; Templeton, A.; Batson, J.; Chen, B.; Jermyn, A.; Conerly, T.; Turner, N.; Anil, C.; Denison, C.; Askell, A.; Lasenby, R.; Wu, Y.; Kravec, S.; Schiefer, N.; Maxwell, T.; Joseph, N.; Hatfield-Dodds, Z.; Tamkin, A.; Nguyen, K.; McLean, B.; Burke, J.E.; Hume, T.; Carter, S.; Henighan, T.; Olah, C.
key_finding: Sparse autoencoders recover interpretable monosemantic features from a 1-layer transformer; features form sparse overcomplete basis
hypothesis_generated: SAE feature firings give natural unit set for avalanche analysis — active SAE features per token ≈ avalanche events

title: Scaling monosemanticity: extracting interpretable features from Claude 3 Sonnet
authors: Templeton, A.; Conerly, T.; Marcus, J.; Lindsey, J.; Bricken, T.; Chen, B.; Pearce, A.; Citro, C.; Ameisen, E.; Jones, A.; Cunningham, H.; Turner, N.; McDougall, C.; MacDiarmid, M.; Tamkin, A.; Durmus, E.; Hume, T.; Mosconi, F.; Freeman, C.D.; Sumers, T.R.; Rees, E.; Batson, J.; Jermyn, A.; Carter, S.; Olah, C.; Henighan, T.
key_finding: Scaling SAEs to Claude 3 Sonnet recovers millions of features including safety-relevant ones
hypothesis_generated: Feature count scales with model; test whether SAE-feature-firing distribution exhibits power laws

title: Gemma Scope: open sparse autoencoders everywhere all at once on Gemma 2
authors: Lieberum, T.; Rajamanoharan, S.; Conmy, A.; Smith, L.; Sonnerat, N.; Varma, V.; Kramár, J.; Dragan, A.; Shah, R.; Nanda, N.
key_finding: Open-weights SAE suite trained on every layer/site of Gemma-2 2B/9B
hypothesis_generated: Public SAEs available for layer-resolved criticality analysis — no SAE training needed

title: Sparse autoencoders find highly interpretable features in language models
authors: Cunningham, H.; Ewart, A.; Riggs, L.; Huben, R.; Sharkey, L.
key_finding: Dictionary learning recovers more interpretable features than raw neurons in Pythia-70M/410M
hypothesis_generated: Independent replication of SAE method; validates feature-firing-as-avalanche-unit assumption

title: Locating and editing factual associations in GPT (ROME)
authors: Meng, K.; Bau, D.; Andonian, A.; Belinkov, Y.
key_finding: Causal-trace experiments localise factual knowledge to mid-layer MLPs in GPT; rank-one updates can edit facts
hypothesis_generated: Causal tracing is gold-standard intervention protocol — adopt for avalanche-propagation experiments

title: Localizing model behaviour with path patching
authors: Goldowsky-Dill, N.; MacLeod, C.; Sato, L.; Arora, A.
key_finding: Path patching refines activation patching to isolate specific residual-stream paths
hypothesis_generated: Methodology: which subcomponents of a forward pass are causally relevant for downstream activation

title: Toy models of superposition
authors: Elhage, N.; Hume, T.; Olsson, C.; Schiefer, N.; Henighan, T.; Kravec, S.; Hatfield-Dodds, Z.; Lasenby, R.; Drain, D.; Chen, C.; Grosse, R.; McCandlish, S.; Kaplan, J.; Amodei, D.; Wattenberg, M.; Olah, C.
key_finding: Networks represent more features than they have neurons via non-orthogonal superposition; phase transitions between monosemantic and polysemantic regimes
hypothesis_generated: Superposition transitions are direct phase transitions — relate to criticality in feature-firing distribution

title: Not all language model features are linear
authors: Engels, J.; Liao, I.; Michaud, E.J.; Gurnee, W.; Tegmark, M.
key_finding: Some LLM features are inherently multi-dimensional (e.g. days of the week as a circle)
hypothesis_generated: Feature geometry is non-trivial; avalanche clusters should be computed in appropriate feature geometry

title: Universal neurons in GPT2 language models
authors: Gurnee, W.; Horsley, T.; Guo, Z.C.; Varma Manoj, T.; Tigges, C.; Kamath, U.; Bertsimas, D.
key_finding: Small fraction (~1-5 percent) of GPT-2 neurons are universal across random seeds — they implement canonical functions (deletion, entropy control)
hypothesis_generated: Universal neurons may be the 'hubs' in a scale-free neuron graph

title: Finding neurons in a haystack: case studies with sparse probing
authors: Gurnee, W.; Nanda, N.; Pauly, M.; Harvey, K.; Troitskii, D.; Bertsimas, D.
key_finding: Sparse probes (k=1-32) find features that are surprisingly often localised to individual neurons in Pythia
hypothesis_generated: Localisation vs distributed coding is a tunable property — relates to avalanche localisation

title: The lazy neuron phenomenon: on emergence of activation sparsity in transformers
authors: Li, Z.; You, C.; Bhojanapalli, S.; Li, D.; Rawat, A.S.; Reddi, S.J.; Ye, K.; Chern, F.; Yu, F.; Guo, R.; Kumar, S.
key_finding: Trained transformers exhibit extreme activation sparsity — only tens of neurons active per token in MLP layers
hypothesis_generated: Activation sparsity implies avalanche-size distributions are highly non-uniform; baseline for 'fraction active' statistic

title: ReLU strikes back: exploiting activation sparsity in large language models
authors: Mirzadeh, I.; Alizadeh, K.; Mehta, S.; Del Mundo, C.C.; Tuzel, O.; Samei, G.; Rastegari, M.; Farajtabar, M.
key_finding: Replacing SiLU/GELU with ReLU in LLMs preserves quality while restoring ~95 percent activation sparsity
hypothesis_generated: Activation function choice directly controls dead-unit fraction; impacts definition of avalanche events

title: Deja Vu: contextual sparsity for efficient LLMs at inference time
authors: Liu, Z.; Wang, J.; Dao, T.; Zhou, T.; Yuan, B.; Song, Z.; Kailas, A.; Shrivastava, A.; Zhang, C.; Tian, Y.; Re, C.; Chen, B.
key_finding: Context-dependent activation sparsity — only 15-25 percent of MLP neurons contribute to each token; predictable from residual stream
hypothesis_generated: Sparsity pattern is token-dependent — avalanches are context-conditional, not static

title: MoEfication: transformer feed-forward layers are mixtures of experts
authors: Zhang, Z.; Lin, Y.; Liu, Z.; Li, P.; Sun, M.; Zhou, J.
key_finding: Dense FFN layers can be decomposed into sparsely-activated expert groups; suggests inherent modularity of FFN computation
hypothesis_generated: Modular structure implies block-avalanche dynamics rather than homogeneous branching

title: Deep residual learning for image recognition
authors: He, K.; Zhang, X.; Ren, S.; Sun, J.
key_finding: Residual connections allow training of 152-layer networks; identity shortcuts mitigate degradation
hypothesis_generated: Skip connections are the central topology knob in the nanoGPT sweep — provide canonical citation

title: Residual networks behave like ensembles of relatively shallow networks
authors: Veit, A.; Wilber, M.; Belongie, S.
key_finding: ResNets can be unrolled into an ensemble of O(2^L) paths with geometrically-distributed effective depths; most gradient flows through short paths
hypothesis_generated: Effective-depth distribution is an avalanche-like statistic; direct precedent

title: Deep learning systems as complex networks
authors: Testolin, A.; Piccolini, M.; Suweis, S.
key_finding: Trained DBNs analysed as graphs exhibit small-world and approximately scale-free connectivity
hypothesis_generated: Direct precedent for treating a trained net as a complex network; look for transformer analogue

title: Structure and performance of fully connected neural networks: emerging complex network properties
authors: Scabini, L.F.S.; Bruno, O.M.
key_finding: Weight-graph degree distributions of trained MLPs show power-law-like tails; structure correlates with generalisation
hypothesis_generated: Weight-graph topology is a candidate observable alongside activation graphs

title: Preferential attachment during the evolution of a potential energy landscape
authors: Watanabe, C.; Hiramatsu, K.; Kashino, K.
key_finding: Effective connectivity in trained deep networks exhibits scale-free structure consistent with preferential attachment during training
hypothesis_generated: VERIFY venue — Front. Comp. Neuro. 2020; training as a growth process producing scale-free topology

title: The critical brain hypothesis in deep learning
authors: Torres, J.J.; Morales, G.B.; di Santo, S.; Muñoz, M.A.
key_finding: VERIFY exact authors/venue — claims that RNN-type networks organise near criticality during training
hypothesis_generated: Direct precedent for criticality-in-trained-nets claim; check methodology

title: Quasi-universal scaling in mouse-brain neuronal activity stems from edge-of-instability critical dynamics
authors: Morales, G.B.; di Santo, S.; Muñoz, M.A.
key_finding: VERIFY — unifies edge-of-instability framework; cross-over between RNN dynamics and cortical activity
hypothesis_generated: Framework may extend to transformer activations; bridge neuroscience and ML

title: Fundamentals of recurrent neural network (RNN) and long short-term memory (LSTM) network
authors: Sherstinsky, A.
key_finding: Comprehensive derivation of RNN/LSTM from differential-equation perspective
hypothesis_generated: Background for treating transformer as dynamical system (via unrolled state)

title: Contrasting random and learned features in deep Bayesian linear regression
authors: Zavatone-Veth, J.A.; Canatar, A.; Ruben, B.S.; Pehlevan, C.
key_finding: Analytical comparison of random vs trained features in deep Bayesian linear nets; feature-learning improves generalisation in a quantifiable regime
hypothesis_generated: Distinguishes 'random-feature' null model from genuine feature-learning — critical for null-hypothesis design

title: Transformer feed-forward layers are key-value memories
authors: Geva, M.; Schuster, R.; Berant, J.; Levy, O.
key_finding: FFN layers in transformers implement key-value memory lookups where keys detect patterns and values promote token distributions
hypothesis_generated: FFN activations have specific semantic structure — natural boundary for defining avalanche events

title: Knowledge neurons in pretrained transformers
authors: Dai, D.; Dong, L.; Hao, Y.; Sui, Z.; Chang, B.; Wei, F.
key_finding: Identifies knowledge neurons whose ablation specifically removes factual associations
hypothesis_generated: Knowledge neurons are candidate 'hubs' — highly connected in functional-activation graph

title: Eight things to know about large language models
authors: Bowman, S.R.
key_finding: Overview of LLM phenomenology including predictable scaling and unpredictable capability emergence
hypothesis_generated: Situates emergence debate; useful context

title: On the theory of implicit deep learning: global convergence with implicit layers
authors: Bai, S.; Kolter, J.Z.; Koltun, V.
key_finding: VERIFY — foundational implicit/deep-equilibrium models paper
hypothesis_generated: Deep-equilibrium nets are naturally fixed-point systems — Lyapunov analysis is immediate

title: Pathways to emergence: universality and criticality in deep networks
authors: Pilarski, S.; et al.
key_finding: VERIFY author list and venue; Jacobian-spectrum proxies for maximal Lyapunov exponent in wide feedforward nets
hypothesis_generated: Direct methodology for Lyapunov-in-deep-net; central citation for sensitivity theme

title: Residual stream analysis with multi-layer SAEs
authors: Zhang, Z.; et al.
key_finding: VERIFY authors and venue; decomposes residual stream across multiple layers into aligned SAE features
hypothesis_generated: Multi-layer SAE needed to track cross-layer avalanches

title: The implicit bias of batch normalization: batch normalization biases residual blocks towards the identity function
authors: De, S.; Smith, S.L.
key_finding: VERIFY exact title; BN initialises residual blocks near identity, keeping effective depth low early in training
hypothesis_generated: Effective-depth / branching connection: BN may hold networks near a 'cold' edge of chaos early in training
